topic: Someone discovers their friend has been lying about their job
raw_output:
[story 1]

The coffee mug felt like a lead weight in my hand. I stared at the printed email, the lines of text blurring into an incomprehensible mess. Sarah had told me for months she was a senior architect at a prestigious firm downtown. Now, this document, an official-looking letterhead, laid bare the truth: she was working as a barista in a cramped, brightly lit cafe across town. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck, turning my cheeks a deep crimson. I slammed the laptop shut, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet apartment. My jaw was clenched so tight I could feel the strain in my temples. "You absolute idiot," I whispered, the words tasting like ash. I paced the small living room, my footsteps heavy and uneven, kicking at the rug as if trying to physically shake the deception out of the air.

[story 2]

I found the discrepancy tucked away in a shared cloud drive, a blurry screenshot of a shift schedule that didn't align with the career trajectory Sarah had painted for me. A sudden, sharp intake of breath hitched in my chest, and my hands started trembling uncontrollably. It wasn't just the facts; it was the calculated omission, the deliberate construction of a better narrative. I felt a sudden, intense pressure building behind my eyes, a hot, stinging sensation that made my vision swim. I walked to the window, gripping the sill until my knuckles turned white. The city lights below seemed aggressively bright, mocking my sudden, internal turmoil. I turned back, my gaze fixed on Sarah, who was humming cheerfully about weekend plans, and the smile felt like a physical assault.

[story 3]

It took three days of silent, simmering fury before I finally confronted her. When I laid out the evidence—the forged documents, the contradictory testimonies—my voice came out unnervingly level, almost too calm. But the tightness around my mouth betrayed the storm raging inside. Every syllable was measured, precise, designed to inflict maximum discomfort. Sarah’s face shifted from breezy denial to a pale, stricken look, and I watched the fragile composure crumble. A deep, visceral churning started in my stomach, a restless energy demanding release. I leaned over the table, my shadow engulfing hers, and I didn't raise my voice, but the sheer force of my disappointment vibrated in the air between us.
